intro: A study across 107 outlets found that major AI models fail basic Stroop test psychology tasks, revealing gaps in their executive control mechanisms. Meanwhile, AI adoption in financial markets is accelerating—hedge funds increasingly use the technology for credit trading, though Barclays reports human traders remain essential. The AI data center boom is straining U.S. power infrastructure, with calls to restructure PJM Interconnection, which operates the grid across 13 states.

whitelist.title: 'New study reveals top AI models completely fail classic 'Stroop' psychological attention test': a fundamental flaw in AI thinking exposed?
whitelist.desc: Executive control mechanisms are key

whitelist.title: Artificial intelligence: Latest News and Updates | South China Morning Post
whitelist.desc: News articles, analyses and commentary about machines that mimic human cognitive functions such as learning and problem solving. This also covers chatbots, algorithms, consumer products and Big Data analysis that make use of AI.

whitelist.title: AI Isn’t Replacing Credit Hedge Fund Traders Yet, Barclays Says
whitelist.desc: Hedge funds and asset managers are increasingly using artificial intelligence when investing in global credit markets, but the technology isn’t replacing human traders — at least for now, according to a survey by Barclays Plc.

whitelist.title: How Much AI Is Too Much for Stock Investors?
whitelist.desc: The latest earnings season is drawing to a close, and it’s revealed how the artificial intelligence boom is engulfing more and more of the S&P 500 Index. The profits are pouring in, yet the US is increasingly a single-narrative market — one where stocks will swoon together when the music stops playing.

whitelist.title: AI Data Center Boom Risks Breakup of Biggest US Power Grid Operator
whitelist.desc: Soaring utility bills and sluggish bureaucracy are fueling calls to split up PJM Interconnection, which runs the grid in 13 states.

whitelist.title: UK forces Google to offer AI search opt-out for publishers

whitelist.title: “If I could snap my fingers and make generative AI disappear forever, I probably would.”
whitelist.desc: Young people do hate AI. “Creatively, I get no enjoyment from using those tools. It defeats the purpose entirely for me,” 20-year-old director of A24’s horror hit Backrooms told The Australian. “To me, generative AI feels less like innovation than a symptom of a broader cultural and economic rot.” Read more about Backrooms on The Verge: [Link: Backrooms is at the forefront of horror’s YouTube wave | https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/938437/backrooms-youtube-kane-parsons-a12 | The Verge]
